region_of_origin: United States (Tex-Mex tradition), served in Singapore

allergens: none identified (check specific restaurant preparation for cross-contamination)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Spicy Beef Taco Soup is approximately 400 g (one bowl), as typically served as a meal at Subway Singapore and similar fast-casual outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Beef and vegetable soup, home-prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 06016) and USDA Foundation Foods ground beef 80/20 (FDC ID 748967), combined with taco seasoning and bean components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for 'Spicy Beef Taco Soup'. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy beef vegetable soup (NDB 06016) as the base matrix with adjustments for ground beef content, kidney beans, black beans, corn, and taco seasoning. Ground beef 80/20 (USDA FDC 748967) and canned kidney beans (USDA FDC 175200) were used as ingredient anchors. The resulting per-100g profile reflects a typical restaurant-style taco soup at approximately 60–65% moisture. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021; analogous estimate based on mixed beef and legume soup
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Spicy Beef Taco Soup. GI estimated at 38 based on analogous mixed legume and beef soup entries from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Kidney beans (GI ~29) and black beans (GI ~30) are the dominant carbohydrate contributors and substantially lower the overall GI. Corn contributes moderate GI (~52) but is a minor component. The blended estimate of GI 38 is consistent with published mixed bean soup analogues. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (38 × 31.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 11.9, rounded to 11.6 after moisture adjustment.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1680 mg per 400 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single 400 g serving provides approximately 1680 mg sodium, representing 73% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2300 mg, and portion reduction or a low-sodium preparation is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~38) due to legume content, but moderate glycemic load per serving (~11.6) and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high legume content (kidney beans, black beans) confers a low GI of approximately 38, making this soup a relatively favourable carbohydrate choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, portion size should be monitored as a full 400 g serving delivers approximately 31 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~11.6.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ground beef (~1.3 g per 100 g) and high sodium content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content from ground beef (approximately 5.2 g per serving) and the high sodium load (~1680 mg per serving) are both relevant cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should request a lean-beef or reduced-sodium preparation and limit serving frequency.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Beef Taco Soup is a hearty, Tex-Mex-inspired soup dish made by simmering seasoned ground beef with kidney beans, black beans, corn, diced tomatoes, and beef broth, flavoured with taco seasoning, chilli powder, cumin, garlic, and onion. The result is a thick, richly spiced soup with a savoury, mildly smoky heat and a satisfying combination of tender beef, creamy beans, and sweet corn. In Singapore, this dish is offered at fast-casual Western outlets such as Subway and mix-and-match food stalls, typically served as a meal bowl priced around SGD 12.90. Nutritionally, a 400 g serving provides approximately 328 kcal, 24.8 g protein, 14 g fat, and 31 g carbohydrate. The legume content (kidney and black beans) contributes significant dietary fibre (~8.4 g per serving) and lowers the overall glycemic index to approximately 38, making it a relatively blood-glucose-friendly option compared to rice- or bread-based meals. However, the sodium content is high (~1680 mg per serving), which is a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is dairy-free and suitable for halal diets when prepared with certified halal beef. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the beef content.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Spicy Beef Taco Soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy beef vegetable soup as a base matrix with ingredient-level adjustments for ground beef, kidney beans, black beans, corn, and taco seasoning. GI is an analogous estimate from published legume soup data. Confidence is medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (beef vegetable soup NDB 06016) and ingredient-level anchors (ground beef 80/20 FDC 748967; kidney beans FDC 175200); no exact match exists in any approved primary source. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy beef soup source entry and is not a characteristic nutrient of this dish. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source matrix used for estimation. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy beef soup source entry. 5. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate based on mixed legume and beef soup entries from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021 AJCN); no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found. 6. The input record listed cuisine as 'Sandwich' and restaurants including 'Subway - Funan'; the cuisine has been corrected to 'Western / Tex-Mex' as taco soup is not a sandwich product. 7. Halal status is listed as false at the record level as halal certification of the specific restaurant preparation cannot be confirmed from approved sources; consumers should verify with the restaurant directly. 8. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate for a standard meal bowl; actual restaurant portion may vary.
